findings.title: Verifier has no withdrawal path, permanently locking the prefunded bankroll and any recovered assets

best_agent_match.title: All funded ETH and recovered tokens are permanently trapped in the verifier

findings.title: Anyone can trigger the strategy against the verifier treasury

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can trigger the strategy against the contract’s treasury

findings.title: Zero-minimum-output swaps let MEV searchers siphon away most of the extracted value

best_agent_match.title: Zero-slippage swaps allow MEV sandwiching to steal nearly all extracted value

findings.title: One-second AMM deadlines make the strategy trivially censorable

best_agent_match.title: One-second swap deadlines make execution trivially censorable

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded mainnet addresses can burn funds on the wrong chain
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on deploying the verifier to an unintended chain; under the intended mainnet deployment assumptions it is an operational misconfiguration risk rather than a reportable protocol flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited approval to TARGET lets the external target pull all verifier AAVE
rejected_candidates.reason: The code does grant an unlimited allowance, but the exploitability claim depends on unsupported assumptions about external TARGET behavior that are not evidenced in this repository.

rejected_candidates.title: Counter exposes unrestricted state mutation
rejected_candidates.reason: `Counter` is a standalone toy contract with no privileged state, no integrations, and no realistic protocol-level impact from public mutation.
